learnedContext: Liked a couple of clean OpenClaw mentions and skipped obvious promo/coin spam; keyboard navigation plus quick content scanning works well for high-signal searches like this.
Using X search + Latest with keyboard focus (j/l) is still the fastest flow; lots of immediate spam/coin posts show up on very fresh results, so skipping aggressively is necessary.

X search in Latest + j/l flow stays fast; the main issue is keyboard focus is ambiguous without browser_get_focused_item, so it’s safer to confirm focus before liking to avoid accidental likes on unrelated/spam posts.

Search+Latest flow loaded correctly and account was confirmed; key limitation was sparse/sticky timeline snapshots making safe per-tweet triage slower, so conservative skipping remains the right default to avoid accidental political/spam engagement.

Login verification via account menu and search setup worked smoothly; the key friction was maintaining strict plan-state continuity in-tool flow, so I should complete all required action+reporting steps before yielding.
